ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cappuccino entry (FOODON_03301737); no specific cocoa-dusted or instant cappuccino variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific cappuccino-with-cocoa granule code exists. Closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for cappuccino with cocoa; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: instant powder dissolved in hot water

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 25 g (one sachet) of Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa granules dissolved in hot water, yielding roughly 200 mL of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa (cr_id G00010436). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from total fat composition typical of milk-powder-based instant cappuccino products; monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat data are not reported in the HPB record and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk-Based Coffee Beverages — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for cappuccino / latte-style milk-coffee beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa granule. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for milk-based coffee beverages (cappuccino/latte category, GI ~51–55). The low carbohydrate content (2.75 g/100 g) means glycemic load per 25 g serving is approximately 0.4, which is negligible. Estimation is conservative and defensible given the milk-powder and minimal sugar composition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (23 mg/100 g) with meaningful potassium offset (100 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is low in sodium and provides potassium from milk solids, making it suitable for hypertensive individuals when consumed without added sugar; however, caffeine from espresso can cause acute blood pressure elevation and intake should be limited to 1–2 cups per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (2.75 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.46 g/100 g) with negligible glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate and sugar content result in a negligible glycemic load (~0.4 per serving), making this beverage acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or flavoured syrups.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.62 g/100 g) and low cholesterol (4 mg/100 g) with cocoa polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and cholesterol content, combined with cocoa flavanols and coffee chlorogenic acids that have evidence for modest cardiovascular benefit, make this beverage low-concern for cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar and limited to moderate daily intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Cocoa polyphenols and coffee chlorogenic acids with anti-inflammatory evidence; low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened cocoa provides flavanols and espresso provides chlorogenic acids with anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; ensure no added sugar is used to preserve the anti-inflammatory nutritional profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cappuccino With Cocoa is an Indonesian-origin instant coffee beverage product, specifically the Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa Granule, widely available in Singapore and Southeast Asia. It is prepared by dissolving a single sachet (approximately 25 g) of granulated powder in hot water, producing a creamy, mildly sweet coffee drink with a cocoa-dusted flavour profile. The beverage combines instant espresso-style coffee, milk powder, cocoa powder, and a small amount of sugar, yielding a warm drink with a smooth, lightly chocolatey taste and a frothy, cappuccino-like texture. Nutritionally, the prepared beverage is low in energy (27 kcal/100 g), low in carbohydrates (2.75 g/100 g), and low in fat (0.99 g/100 g), with a modest protein contribution (1.71 g/100 g) from milk solids. Sodium is very low at 23 mg/100 g, and potassium at 100 mg/100 g provides a meaningful mineral offset. The cocoa component contributes flavanols with anti-inflammatory and potential insulin-sensitising properties, while coffee chlorogenic acids add further antioxidant value. The glycemic load per serving is negligible (~0.4), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular risk when consumed without additional sugar. It is classified as ultra-processed due to its granule formulation but remains nutritionally modest in macronutrient density.
common_user_queries: Is Good Day Cappuccino with Cocoa healthy?
How many calories in a sachet of Good Day Cappuccino?
Can diabetics drink cappuccino with cocoa?
Is cappuccino with cocoa suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of Good Day Cappuccino?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010436, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. The input record is marked as reviewed with canonical provenance. Saturated fat is estimated from typical milk-powder product composition and documented accordingly.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; this is consistent with an instant beverage product with minimal fibre-contributing ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this instant cappuccino granule product are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g (0.62 g/100 g) is estimated from the total fat value using a typical saturated-to-total-fat ratio for milk-powder-based instant coffee products (~63%), consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for similar products; this is an adapted value and documented as such. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 53, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database milk-based coffee beverage category; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. The primary_cuisine is listed as Indonesian in the input, reflecting the Good Day brand origin (PT Santos Jaya Abadi, Indonesia); Indonesian local name is provided accordingly per G6 multilingual rules.
